food_subgroup: Hot and cold kopitiam beverages — bundle serving

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a bundled kopitiam drinks set. FOODON_03301710 (beverage food product) used as closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A003K (Coffee and coffee substitutes) used as closest parent; the bundle typically contains kopi (local coffee) and teh (local tea) in XL format. No bundle-level FoodEx2 code exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept maps to a bundled kopitiam beverage set.

serving.description: 1 bundle of 4 XL drinks (approx. 400 ml each)
serving.natural_language_portion: One bundle consists of four XL-sized kopitiam beverages (approximately 400 ml each, totalling approximately 1,600 ml), typically a combination of kopi (local coffee with condensed milk) and teh (local tea with condensed milk) as served at Toast Box.

nutrient_source.title: Kopi (local coffee with condensed milk) and Teh (local tea with condensed milk) — nutrient composition per 100 ml
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) — Foodo / Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: The bundle contains 4 XL kopitiam drinks. Nutrient values per 100 g (ml) are estimated as a weighted average of kopi (coffee with condensed milk) and teh (tea with condensed milk) as reported in the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database. Typical Toast Box XL drinks are approximately 400 ml each. The per-100 ml composite reflects a 50/50 blend assumption of kopi and teh, consistent with standard kopitiam bundle offerings. Energy and macros are dominated by condensed milk and sugar content.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sweetened condensed milk beverages and sugar-sweetened hot drinks — analogous estimate based on sweetened coffee/tea GI data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Toast Box kopi or teh. GI of 55 is estimated based on University of Sydney GI Database data for sweetened condensed-milk coffee and tea beverages, which report GI values in the range 50–60. A GI of 55 is classified as 'low'. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 140.8 g) / 100 = 77.44, rounded to 77.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content and caffeine from coffee and tea
hypertension.guidance: Each XL kopitiam drink contributes approximately 28 mg sodium per 100 ml; the full bundle of 1,600 ml delivers approximately 448 mg sodium, and the caffeine in kopi and teh may transiently elevate blood pressure, so individuals with hypertension should limit intake to one drink and choose lower-sugar variants.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar load from condensed milk and added sugar across four XL servings
type2_diabetes.guidance: The full 4-drink bundle delivers approximately 140 g of carbohydrate (predominantly sucrose and lactose from condensed milk), representing a very high glycemic load; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this bundle or substitute with unsweetened kopi-o or teh-o variants to eliminate condensed milk sugar.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and sugar from condensed milk across a large-volume bundle
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The condensed milk in traditional kopi and teh contributes saturated fat (approximately 0.9 g per 100 ml) and high sugar; consuming the full 4-drink XL bundle regularly may contribute to elevated LDL cholesterol and triglycerides, and individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit to one drink and request reduced-sugar or evaporated-milk substitutions.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Toast Box XL Drinks Bundle (4 drinks) is a promotional beverage bundle offered by Toast Box, a Singaporean kopitiam-style café chain, consisting of four extra-large (approximately 400 ml each) traditional Singaporean hot or iced drinks. The bundle typically includes combinations of kopi (Singaporean-style robusta coffee brewed with a cloth sock filter and blended with sweetened condensed milk), teh (Ceylon or local black tea brewed strong and blended with condensed milk), kopi-o (black coffee with sugar), and teh-o (black tea with sugar). Toast Box is known for its heritage Nanyang coffee culture, and the XL format is designed for sharing or large-appetite customers. Nutritionally, the dominant macronutrient is carbohydrate, primarily from sucrose and lactose in condensed milk, contributing approximately 8.8 g per 100 ml and a very high glycemic load across the full bundle. Fat content is modest at approximately 1.3 g per 100 ml, largely saturated from dairy. Protein is approximately 1.4 g per 100 ml from milk solids. The bundle is high in added sugar and is clinically significant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or obesity. Sodium is moderate at approximately 28 mg per 100 ml. The bundle is halal-certified and vegetarian-suitable but not vegan due to dairy. It is a culturally iconic Singaporean beverage experience rooted in the Hainanese coffee-shop tradition.

confidence_reason: No direct nutrient analysis exists for the Toast Box XL Drinks Bundle as a packaged product. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Singapore Food Composition Database entries for kopi (coffee with condensed milk) and teh (tea with condensed milk), which are the closest approved-source matches. The bundle composition (4 XL drinks, approximately 400 ml each) is inferred from field survey listing context and Toast Box menu conventions. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database data for sweetened condensed-milk beverages. Confidence is medium due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: 1. This record represents a promotional bundle (15% off, 4x XL drinks) from Toast Box - Funan as listed on field survey data. The food_name has been canonicalised to remove the promotional discount prefix. 2. Nutrient values per 100 g (ml) are adapted from HPB Foodo database entries for kopi and teh; a 50/50 blend assumption is applied. Actual composition may vary depending on the specific drinks chosen within the bundle. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as brewed coffee and tea beverages with condensed milk contain no dietary fibre per HPB source data. 4. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per HPB source data for kopi and teh. 5. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as coffee and tea beverages with condensed milk contain negligible ascorbic acid. 6. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database for kopi/teh entries. 7. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database for kopi/teh entries. 8. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database for kopi/teh entries. 9. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database for kopi/teh entries. 10. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database for kopi/teh entries. 11. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database for kopi/teh entries. 12. Glycemic load per serving is calculated as (GI 55 × carbohydrate per serving 140.8 g) / 100 = 77.44, rounded to 77.4. The gi_category is 'low' as GI 55 falls within the low GI range (55 or less). 13. Price SGD 23.60 preserved from input (15% off promotional price). 14. Cuisine categorised as Singaporean Kopitiam reflecting the Toast Box brand identity.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Canonicalised food_name by removing promotional discount prefix '(15% off)'.
Iteration 1: Added full Singaporean multilingual names (Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) to local_names.
Iteration 1: Built comprehensive aliases and aliases_by_language with consistency verification.
Iteration 1: Adapted nutrient values from HPB Foodo kopi and teh entries as closest approved-source match.
Iteration 1: Documented all -1 sentinel values with explicit reasons in notes.
Iteration 1: Documented all 0.0 values (dietary_fibre_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg) with 'confirmed zero' justification in notes.
Iteration 1: Set GI to 55 (medium) as analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for sweetened condensed-milk beverages.

Iteration 2: Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' for glycemic_index 55, as per University of Sydney GI Database classification (GI <= 55 is low). Updated gi_source.source_match_notes and notes accordingly.
Iteration 2: Added the romanized alias 'Tu Si Gong Fang Chao Da Bei Yin Liao Tao Can' to the flat 'aliases' array to ensure consistency with 'aliases_by_language.romanized', resolving the q8 failure.
Iteration 2: Verified ontology match_type values are strictly 'exact', 'closest_parent', or 'none'.
Iteration 2: Confirmed g4 zero-macro justification phrases present in notes for all 0.0 macro-adjacent values.
